ingredients.label_expression:
Potassium citrate
equivalent potassium (K)
ingredients.tga_restrictions: When used as an active ingredient and the medicine is intended as a mineral supplementation, potassium is a mandatory component of potassium citrate.
ingredients.max_dose_justification: No ULs have been set for potassium from dietary sources. AI is 3,800 mg https://www.eatforhealth.gov.au/sites/default/files/2022-04/n35-potassium_0.pdf

clinical_details.introduction:
Potassium is a key element in the body forming a large percentage of the ionic component of the environment inside cells [intracellular] as opposed to the extracellular environment [in plasma and outside of and between cells. 

The body has a daily obligate loss of potassium through the kidneys since the kidneys are not perfectly efficient at reabsorbing potassium from the renal tubules in the process of filtering the blood.

Low potassium values can come from endocrine diseases such as Addison’s disease and hypoaldosteronism. Low potassium can also occur in patients who lose extra potassium through the kidneys through the use of medications such as diuretics. [Reference 7]

Standard Western diets are higher in sodium than potassium. This increased ratio of sodium to potassium results in an increased risk of high blood pressure, hypertension and stroke. [Reference 1, 2, 11]
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Excessive potassium levels can result in cardiac problems including bradycardia, low blood pressure and in extreme cases death. Oral potassium can also cause nausea and gastric irritation.

ingredients.label_expression:
Copper gluconate
equivalent copper (Cu)
ingredients.tga_restrictions:
Copper is a mandatory component of copper gluconate.
The percentage of copper from copper gluconate should be calculated based on the molecular weight of copper gluconate.
When for internal use the maximum daily dose must not contain more than 5 mg of copper.
When for other than internal use, the concentration of copper compounds must 
be no more than 5%.
ingredients.max_dose_justification: Upper level of 10 mg Cu Elemental daily assigned. https://www.eatforhealth.gov.au/sites/default/files/2022-04/n35-copper_0.pdf TR 1-6mg copper daily UL for adults 10 mg daily elemental copper Happy with TR 1-5 mg of elemental copper

clinical_details.introduction:
Copper, an essential mineral, is naturally present in some foods and is available as a dietary supplement. It is a cofactor for several enzymes (known as “cuproenzymes”) involved in energy production, iron metabolism, neuropeptide activation, connective tissue synthesis, and neurotransmitter synthesis [1-3]. One abundant cuproenzyme is ceruloplasmin (CP), which plays a role in iron metabolism and carries more than 95% of the total copper in healthy human plasma [4]. Copper is also involved in many physiologic processes, such as angiogenesis; neurohormone homeostasis; and regulation of gene expression, brain development, pigmentation, and immune system functioning [1]. In addition, defense against oxidative damage depends mainly on the copper-containing superoxide dismutases [5,6].
A wide variety of plant and animal foods contain copper, and the average human diet provides approximately 1,400 mcg/day for men and 1,100 mcg/day for women that is primarily absorbed in the upper small intestine [1,2,7-9]. Almost two-thirds of the body’s copper is located in the skeleton and muscle [1,3].

Only small amounts of copper are typically stored in the body, and the average adult has a total body content of 50–120 mg copper [1,2]. Most copper is excreted in bile, and a small amount is excreted in urine. Total fecal losses of copper of biliary origin and nonabsorbed dietary copper are about 1 mg/day [1,2]. Copper levels in the body are homeostatically maintained by copper absorption from the intestine and copper release by the liver into bile to provide protection from copper deficiency and toxicity [3].
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks:
Chronic exposure to high levels of copper can result in liver damage and gastrointestinal symptoms (e.g., abdominal pain, cramps, nausea, diarrhea, and vomiting) [10,24]. Copper toxicity is rare in healthy individuals who do not have a hereditary copper homeostasis defect. However, copper toxicity has been reported in people who consume water containing high levels of copper as a result of stagnant water in copper-containing pipes and fixtures as well as copper alloys in water distribution systems and household plumbing that allow copper to leach into water [10,24]. The Environmental Protection Agency has established a recommended upper limit for copper in public water systems of 1.3 mg/L [24,25].

People with Wilson’s disease, a rare, autosomal recessive disease, have a high risk of copper toxicity. Wilson’s disease, which is caused by a mutation in ATP7B, leads to abnormally high tissue levels of copper as a result of defective copper clearance [26]. People with this disease can develop neurologic and liver damage that can result in cirrhosis [1]. Patients can also develop acute hepatitis, hemolytic crisis, and liver failure. Lifelong copper chelation therapy or high doses of zinc can prevent permanent organ damage in these patients.
The FNB has established ULs for copper from food and supplements for healthy individuals based on levels associated with liver damage [10]. The ULs do not apply to individuals who are receiving supplemental copper

ingredients.max_dose_justification:
TR 250 mg - 1500 mg daily TBR 
Higher supplement range 300–800 mg (YSCP).

clinical_details.introduction: GABA is a neurotransmitter in the brain. It has been used for anxiety, panic disorders and stress. It can also be helpful for insomnia and has been found to lower blood pressure.  GABA is an inhibitory neurotransmitter in the brain which promotes calm and prevents overactivity of the brain [1]. It appears to be beneficial in ADD, Attention Deficit Disorder. Limited studies have also shown modest effects on blood pressure, but this is not a significant use for the supplement [2].
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Some of the reported side effects of GABA include fatigue and sleepiness. Stomach distress, nausea, reduced appetite and muscle weakness have been reported.

ingredients.label_expression:
Griffonia simplicifolia seed extract
equivalent Griffonia simplicifolia dry seed
equivalent 5-hydroxytryptophan
ingredients.max_dose_justification: TR 100- 300 mg daily, trials at higher levels of 3250 mg anxiety +800 mg, migraine = 600 mg https://altmedrev.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/02/v7-4-336.pdf?utm

clinical_details.introduction:
5-HTP is an active metabolite of the essential amino acid tryptophan. 5-HTP is also an amino acid but it is not an essential amino acid [1].

5-HTP has been found to have numerous beneficial effects. The most studied use is for depression and to a lesser extent anxiety. It is sedating and has been useful for insomnia as well.
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks:
The most common side effects of 5-HTP are gastroenterological including nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and heartburn. Somnolence is also a possible side effect as would be expected in any agent which may facilitate sleep.
                     There is a risk of a potentially serious or fatal condition called serotonin syndrome with 
                     excessive administration of drugs or other agents that increase serotonin directly or through 
                     reuptake inhibition or through a combination of such mechanisms. Tryptophan and 5-HTP
                     being serotonergic in nature can potentially contribute to the risk of Serotonin Syndrome. 

                     Following an initial large number of cases of Eosinophilia Myalgia syndrome with tryptophan    
                     supplements thought possibly due to a contaminant there have been rare sporadic reports 
                     of this syndrome with both tryptophan and 5-HTP supplements [8,9].

ingredients.label_expression: Theanine
ingredients.tga_restrictions:
Only to be used in a medicine where Trans Chem Pty Ltd (Client ID 21878), who applied to have the ingredient included in this Determination, is the sponsor of the medicine or has given written authorisation to the sponsor of a medicine to include the ingredient in the medicine. This paragraph ceases to be
 a requirement for this ingredient after 1 March 2026.
The route of administration for medicines that contain theanine must be limited to oral.
The maximum recommended daily dose of the medicine must not provide more than 450 mg of theanine.
ingredients.tga_warnings:
The following warning statements (or words to the same effect) are required on
 the medicine label:- (PREGNT) 'Not recommended for use by pregnant and lactating women'; and- (ADULT) 'Adults only'.
ingredients.max_dose_justification: TR 100-400 mg, doses of 600 - 1200 mg have been used.  it is recommended that the maximum dosage not exceed 600 mg in a six-hour period or 1,200 mg in a 24-hour period. https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11811612/?utm TR 100-400 mg daily

clinical_details.introduction: L-theanine is a non-essential amino acid derived from tea. It has the unique property of being able to induce calm awareness without sedation in most people. It has been found to have antianxiety and stress relieving properties. There have also been studies showing some benefit in weight loss and in regard to blood lipid profiles, but these studies are more limited in nature and are not definitive.
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Side effects with l-theanine have been generally mild and may include headache, low blood pressure and sleepiness.

clinical_details.introduction: Arginine is an amino acid found in humans. It has been demonstrated to dilate blood vessels and lower blood pressure. It may also be of value in preventing the condition necrotizing enterocolitis in premature infants and in improving sexual function in men.
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Arginine may slightly lower blood pressure which could be a problem for certain individuals.  Other side effects that have been reported include nausea, diarrhea, abdominal discomfort and precipitation of gout. Allergic reactions and worsening of asthma symptoms have also been noted.

clinical_details.introduction: Citrulline is an amino acid found in humans. It has demonstrated effects on improving blood pressure and also has been used to enhance athletic performance.
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Citrulline has a low reported incidence of side effects. Those most commonly reported include upset stomach, acid reflux, cough and nonspecific urinary symptoms.

ingredients.label_expression: Tyrosine
ingredients.max_dose_justification: 100- 2000 mg  (divided doses). 1000 mg first thing in am, 9gm daily for narcolepsy BC Agreed TR 500-1000mg

clinical_details.introduction:
Tyrosine is a non-essential amino acid, meaning that although it is critical for the formation of neurotransmitters and thyroid hormone production it can be synthesized in the body and does not need to come from the diet exclusively.

Tyrosine is also an important building block of numerous proteins.

Of critical import a key indication for the well documented use of tyrosine is in the inherited condition called PKU – phenylketonuria.  It is also used for cognitive support. [Reference 1,2,3]
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks:
The most common side effects are nausea, headache, fatigue, and heartburn.

Since the body requires tyrosine to make thyroxine, the key thyroid hormone ingesting extra tyrosine could increase thyroxine levels too much. If this happened conditions such as hyperthyroidism and Graves’ disease could get worse. Hence it is recommended that you should avoid taking tyrosine supplements unless specifically advised to do so by your medical practitioner.

ingredients.label_expression:
Bromelains (from Pineapple)
equivalent bromelains
ingredients.tga_restrictions: May be derived from either the stem or fruit of the pineapple (Ananas comosus).
ingredients.max_dose_justification: TR 200 - 2000 mg day https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC538506/

clinical_details.introduction: Bromelain is a plant derived enzyme. It is frequently given together with other enzyme class supplements. It has been used as a replacement for digestive enzymes and has been studied to speed recovery from surgery and injuries. It has also been studied and used in sinusitis and phlebitis. [1,2,6,11]. It also been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects [7].
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: The most frequently reported side effects include diarrhea, stomach and abdominal pain and allergic reactions.

ingredients.label_expression: Glutamine
ingredients.max_dose_justification: 2-30 gm daily BC

clinical_details.introduction: Glutamine is a non-essential amino acid. It is a compound naturally found in the body. It is important for immune, digestive, and muscle health. The most common use as a dietary supplement is either alone or in combination with other agents to improve gastrointestinal health and integrity. It also may be of value in individuals who have pancreatitis in improving outcomes and decreasing death rates.
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Most side effects with glutamine are minor but they can be troubling. They include nausea, vomiting, gas and stomach pains. Joint or back pain have also been reported as have headache, fatigue, dizziness, and mild skin rash. Dry mouth, runny nose and an increased tendency to sweat have all been observed as well.

ingredients.label_expression:
Calcium beta-hydroxy-beta-methylbutyrate monohydrate
equivalent calcium (Ca)
ingredients.max_dose_justification: TR agreed up to 3gm

clinical_details.introduction: HMB is an amino-acid breakdown product that is naturally created in the body in the metabolism of the branched chain amino acid Leucine. It has been shown to have a broad spectrum of metabolic benefits primarily related to preventing sarcopenia, increasing muscle mass with weight training, decreasing soreness after athletic workouts, improving cardiovascular health and immune function. Additionally, it can have beneficial effects on by promoting weight loss and on fat loss with relative muscle preservation. HMB has also been reported to have beneficial effects on blood pressure and lipid profiles but it is not widely used for these indications.
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: There are no widely recognized specific side effects of this supplement. Taking on an empty stomach may cause stomach upset in susceptible individuals.

ingredients.label_expression: Inositol
ingredients.max_dose_justification: TR 1- 18 gm daily (PMDD/Anxiety) https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/7793450/ and 18 gm https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/8780431/ TR 1000 - 4000 mg daily

clinical_details.introduction: Myo-Inositol (free of phosphate) was once considered a member of the vitamin B group and was called Vitamin B8 until it was clear that it was not an essential nutrient since it could be synthesized by the body from glucose, primarily in the kidneys. A small portion of myo-inositol is converted in peripheral tissues to d-chiro inositol which may have beneficial effects on insulin resistance and hence glucose metabolism. It has also been shown to reduce oxidative stress [1].
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Inositol would usually be considered safe in adults. Side effects are rare and are generally      limited to nausea, stomach pain, fatigue, headache, and dizziness. Most side effects occur with doses greater than 12 g per day. It may lead to hypoglycemia especially in individuals prone to this or on other glucose lowering agents. There has also been concern that it could lead to vitamin and mineral malabsorption, also presumably at high sustained doses.

clinical_details.introduction:
Chiro-Inositol (free of phosphate) was once considered a member of the vitamin B group and was called Vitamin B8 until it was clear that it was not an essential nutrient since it could be synthesized by the body from glucose, primarily in by peripheral conversion of myo-inositol made in the kidneys into the d-chiro form.   D-Chiro inositol may have more specific beneficial effects on insulin resistance and hence glucose metabolism. It has also been shown to reduce oxidative stress [4].
                      When d-chiro inositol is used as a nutritional supplement it is often used in combination
                      with myo-inositol at a ratio of 1:40. Hence you will note that doses listed for d-chiro inositol 
                      are approximately 1/40th those of the myo-inositol doses listed elsewhere.
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Inositol would usually be considered safe in adults. Side effects are rate and are generally      limited to nausea, stomach pain, fatigue, headache, and dizziness. Most side effects occur with doses greater than 12 g per day. It may lead to hypoglycemia especially in individuals prone to this or on other glucose lowering agents. There has also been concern that it could lead to vitamin and mineral malabsorption, also presumably at high sustained doses.

ingredients.label_expression: Acetylcysteine
ingredients.tga_restrictions:
Only for use in topical medicines for dermal application. 
The concentration in the medicine must be no more than 0.001%.
ingredients.max_dose_justification: TR 600 mg to 2400 mg daily https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19581567/

clinical_details.introduction: N-acetyl cysteine (NAC) is a modified version of the amino acid cysteine. Cystine is important in a number of vital metabolic pathways giving and receiving methyl groups involved in everything from protein synthesis to detoxification reactions. Taken by mouth NAC is key to  the activity of the body’s most important antioxidant inside cells called glutathione. It has  been used in a number of different medical conditions with some of the best success being for the treatment of chronic bronchitis. It also appears to be beneficial in the setting of certain kinds of viral infections such as influenza. [1]
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Reported side effects include nausea, diarrhea, fatigue, and rash. Headache, lowered blood pressure, and runny nose have also been noted. It is also reported that extended use of NAC may decrease the absorption of some important trace minerals and in such as setting a trace mineral supplement may be considered.

ingredients.label_expression: Taurine
ingredients.max_dose_justification: 500-2000 mg daily BC

clinical_details.introduction: Taurine is an amino acid like compound. It has been used to increase circulation and blood flow. It has also been used to improve cognitive function and athletic performance. It is a strong antioxidant that can help slow the damage to cells. It is also used in over the counter energy drinks.
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Nausea, vomiting and diarrhea are reported as is constipation.

clinical_details.introduction:
Zinc is an essential mineral that is naturally present in some foods, added to others, and available as a dietary supplement. Zinc is also found in many cold lozenges and some over-the-counter drugs sold as cold remedies.

Zinc is involved in numerous aspects of cellular metabolism. It is required for the catalytic activity of approximately 100 enzymes [1,2] and it plays a role in immune function [3,4], protein synthesis [4], wound healing [5], DNA synthesis [2,4], and cell division [4]. Zinc also supports normal growth and development during pregnancy, childhood, and adolescence [6-8] and is required for proper sense of taste and smell [9]. A daily intake of zinc is required to maintain a steady state because the body has no specialized zinc storage system [10].
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks:
Zinc toxicity can occur in both acute and chronic forms. Acute adverse effects of high zinc intake include nausea, vomiting, loss of appetite, abdominal cramps, diarrhea, and headaches [2]. One case report cited severe nausea and vomiting within 30 minutes of ingesting 4 g of zinc gluconate (570 mg elemental zinc) [50]. Intakes of 150–450 mg of zinc per day have been associated with such chronic effects as low copper status, altered iron function, reduced immune function, and reduced levels of high-density lipoproteins [51]. Reductions in a copper-containing enzyme, a marker of copper status, have been reported with even moderately high zinc intakes of approximately 60 mg/day for up to 10 weeks [2]. The doses of zinc used in the AREDS study (80 mg per day of zinc in the form of zinc oxide for 6.3 years, on average) have been associated with a significant increase in hospitalizations for genitourinary causes, raising the possibility that chronically high intakes of zinc adversely affect some aspects of urinary physiology [52].

Excess zinc in the absence of or when there is an imbalance between availability of copper and zinc can lead to anemia.

ingredients.label_expression:
S-acetyl-l-glutathione
equivalent glutathione
ingredients.max_dose_justification: TR 250 - 1500 mg  2,000 mg oral has been reported in clinical case literature but is not established by randomized dose-finding trials.

clinical_details.introduction: Glutathione is a key if not the key intracellular antioxidant in the human body. It plays a protective role in maintaining cellular health against a wide variety of exogenous and endogenous compounds and stresses. The production of glutathione decreases with age and supplementation has been of benefit in a variety of situations. In addition to its roles in inflammation control glutathione is also important in detoxification pathways.
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Side effects of glutathione include bloating and abdominal cramps. Allergic reactions and respiratory embarrassment due to bronchial constriction are also reported.

The maximum recommended daily dose of the medicine must not provide more than 1200 mg sodium butyrate.

clinical_details.introduction: Short chain fatty acids including butyrate, propionate and acetate are produced by the gut flora from prebiotic fibers. Of these three butyrate is the most important as it serves a key role in both nourishing the colonocytes which burn it for fuel and in sending immunomodulatory signals to balance immune function. Butyrate falls into a class of compounds called post-biotics. Unlike prebiotics which provide fiber to fuel the growth of the microbiome and probiotics which provide live or dead organisms themselves, postbiotics represent supplements that include metabolic products of healthy microbiome metabolism. [References 1,2,3].
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: Butyrate as a supplement has shown virtually no side effects in clinical trials. When butyrate concentrations are increased by adding prebiotic fiber some gassiness and bloating which is usually temporary has been noted.

ingredients.label_expression: 3,3'-diindolylmethane
ingredients.max_dose_justification: 100-300 mg daily

clinical_details.introduction:
Diindolylmethane is derived from cruciferous vegetables as the primary metabolite of the compound Indole 3 Carbinol [I3C] found in brussel sprouts, cabbage, cauliflower, cabbage, and other members of the cruciferous vegetable group. These vegetables are thought to have cellular protective effects on a variety of pathways. I3C is converted to DIM by a plant derived enzyme called myrosinase. Myrosinase is also produced by some but not all people in their gut flora. 

DIM is thought to have a number of positive metabolic effects on differing body systems. It may limit the accumulation of body fat. It has been shown to have immunomodulating effects. It also has antioxidant properties and may protect cells from radiation damage.

Diets that include significant amounts of cruciferous vegetables have shown numerous health benefits. A number of these benefits are believed to be due to I3C and its active metabolite DIM.  [1,2,3,4,5].
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: The most common side effects of DIM are headache, nausea, vomiting and diarrhea. At doses over 600 mg, hyponatremia-low blood sodium levels have been reported.

ingredients.label_expression: Hobamine (Salicylamine acetate)
ingredients.max_dose_justification: TSI dose - See TS Website dose & reference

clinical_details.introduction: 2-hydroxybenzylamine (2-HOBA), is a naturally occurring compound that is at the forefront of new developments to support cellular health [10]. It is naturally found in buckwheat, is orally available, rapidly metabolized, and crosses the blood brain barrier, resulting in brain 2-HOBA levels approximately twice as high as plasma levels [1,2]. 2-HOBA acts as a potent scavenger of reactive aldehyde species (RASP) [5]. RASPs are formed by lipid peroxidation, alcohol oxidation, and polyamine & glucose metabolism and elevated levels of RASPs are generally observed in ocular and systemic inflammatory disease [4]. Recent & registered clinical trials using 2-HOBA show promising effect on multiple cardiometabolic diseases & inflammatory-disease processes [7,8,9].
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: 2-HOBA acetate is safe and well-tolerated at doses from 50 mg and up to 2,175 mg daily in healthy human volunteers [1,3].

ingredients.label_expression: Urolithin A
ingredients.tga_restrictions: The route of administration for medicines that contain urolithin A must be oral. The maximum recommended daily dose of a medicine must not exceed 1000 mg of urolithin A.
ingredients.tga_warnings: Medicines that contain urolithin A must be indicated for use in adults only and not in pregnant or lactating women. The recommended duration for use for a medicine containing urolithin A must be four months or less.
ingredients.max_dose_justification: Agreed,TR 150 -  1000 mg

clinical_details.introduction: Urolithin A is a dietary metabolite of the intestinal microbiota following the ingestion of plant-based food ingredients ellagitannins and ellagic acid (found in berries, nuts, and seeds, and pomegranate in concentrated levels) [2,4]. Urolithin production in the intestine is dependent on the host’s microbiome [9]. It is a natural active substance that has various health benefits, showing decreased oxidative stress, improved cognitive impairment, is anti-inflammatory and anti-aging, activates mitochondrial autophagy, enhances intestinal barrier and exhibiting anti-cancer function [2].
clinical_details.side_effects_and_risks: No known side effects
